mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor demand that translates into higher aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000 thousand units indicates resilient residential investment that propagates through construction, durable goods, and credit channels, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following HOUST > 1000 are negative or not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) in >50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an accommodative nominal policy rate below 0.5% and a concurrent month-over-month increase in industrial production compresses the real cost of capital while output accelerates, producing amplified positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform the simple sum of individual FEDFUNDS and INDPRO signals (Sharpe ratio difference < 0.2) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in retail sales signals that consumer demand is rolling over, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market that immediately reduces near-term recession risk and triggers risk-on repositioning, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following UNRATE < 5.0 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in more than half of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A nominal federal funds rate below 0.5% eliminates short-term Treasury yields and drives a persistent reallocation of institutional capital into dividend-paying equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS < 0.5 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and declines in nonfarm payrolls indicate stagflationary pressure that squeezes real incomes and forces multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while PAYEMS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and falling PAYEMS does not generate significantly negative 60-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered observations.
